query: What is the current status of SpaceX Starship Flight 13 (IFT-13) as of mid-July 2026? Has Booster 20 completed its static fire test? Has the FAA approved Flight 13 / accepted the Flight 12 mishap investigation? What is the latest NET (no earlier than) launch date shown on trackers like NextSpaceflight and RGV Aerial Photography?
answer:
I've completed thorough research on SpaceX Starship Flight 13 (IFT-13) status as of mid-July 2026. Here are the key findings answering each part of your question:

**NET Launch Date:** Flight 13 targets **NET Thursday, July 16, 2026**, with a 90-minute window opening at 5:45 p.m. CT (10:45 p.m. UTC). Earlier in the week some trackers listed July 15, but by July 13 the consensus converged on July 16. NextSpaceflight showed 14/20 checklist items complete.

**Booster static fire — YES, COMPLETE:** The booster for Flight 13 is **Booster 20** (paired with Ship 40). It completed a full 33-engine static fire on **July 10, 2026** — a record ~24-second duration, the last major pre-flight booster test. Ship 40 completed its own static fire earlier (~July 1–2).

**FAA / Flight 12 mishap investigation — CLOSED/CLEARED:** On **July 13, 2026**, the FAA closed its Flight 12 mishap investigation, accepting SpaceX's findings and corrective actions and clearing Flight 13 to proceed. The Flight 12 anomaly (May 22, 2026) involved Booster 19's Raptor 3 engines failing to reignite after stage separation.

**One clarification:** Your question referenced "Booster 20" — that's correct for Flight 13. (Flight 12, which had the mishap, flew Booster 19.) Flight 13 also marks the first operational Starship payload: 20 Starlink V3 satellites.

Full synthesis, sources, and structured data points have been provided.

sources.title: STARSHIP IFT-13, July 16, 2026, Starbase, TX, 6:45 pm ET
sources.snippet: SpaceX will launch Starship's 13th Integrated Flight Test (IFT-13) as soon as Thursday, July 16, 2026, at 5:45 pm Central Time (6:45 pm Eastern).

sources.title: FAA Closes SpaceX Starship Flight 12 Investigation, Clears Flight 13 for Launch
sources.snippet: The FAA closed its investigation into the Starship Flight 12 mishap, clearing SpaceX to proceed with Flight 13; the FAA accepted SpaceX's findings and corrective actions, concluding no public injuries or property damage.

sources.title: Starship Flight 13 Cleared to Launch Thursday with First Functional Starlink V3 Payloads
sources.snippet: The FAA closed its investigation into the Starship Flight 12 booster anomaly on Monday, accepting SpaceX's corrective actions.

sources.title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
sources.snippet: With the 33 engine static fire completed for Booster 20, SpaceX is pushing hard to fly Flight 13 as soon as possible. Booster 20 rolled out to Pad 2 for a 33-engine static fire after a month in Mega Bay 1.

sources.title: SpaceX ignites all 33 powerful engines on Starship booster ahead of Flight 13 launch
sources.snippet: SpaceX on July 10 completed a static fire of the Super Heavy booster; all 33 engines fired. Ship 40 underwent its own static fire on July 2.

sources.title: Booster 20 Fires All 33 Engines for Record 24 Seconds Ahead of Flight 13
sources.snippet: SpaceX ignited all 33 Raptor engines on Booster 20 for a record 24 seconds on July 10, targeting a launch window opening as soon as July 15.

sources.title: SpaceX reveals Starship Flight 13 launch date
sources.snippet: Flight 13 targets Thursday, July 16 with a 90-minute window opening at 5:45 p.m. CT. Uses Booster 20 and Ship 40. Carries 20 Starlink V3 satellites. Flight 12 flew May 22.

sources.title: Starship | Next Spaceflight
sources.snippet: July 13: 14/20 Checklist Items Complete. Booster 20 and Ship 40 proof and static fire campaigns marked; awaiting stacking, FAA Operations Advisory, NOTAMs, maritime notices.

sources.title: FAA requires SpaceX-led mishap investigation before resumption of Starship launches
sources.snippet: On May 27 the FAA determined Flight 12 (May 22) resulted in a mishap requiring investigation before another flight.

sources.title: SpaceX Starship Grounded After Flight 12 Booster Failure: FAA Investigation
sources.snippet: Booster 19's Raptor 3 engines failed to reignite properly after stage separation, sending Super Heavy Booster 19 to a hard splashdown.

sources.title: SpaceX Schedules Starship Flight 13 For July 16 Launch
sources.snippet: SpaceX set to launch Flight 13 on July 16, 90-minute window opening 5:45 p.m. CT; deploys 20 Starlink V3 satellites adding 60 Tbps.

sources.title: Starship Flight 13 / WAI Hub
sources.snippet: Launch Thursday, July 16, 2026 at 10:45 PM UTC. Booster 20, Ship 40. Ship 40 cryo tests May 4-5, 60-second static fire July 1. Booster 20 cryo June 7, 25-second 33-engine static fire July 10.

data_points.metric: Booster 20 engines fired in static fire
FAA closure of Flight 12 mishap investigation date
